reasoning: The Lean definition `g n = Real.sqrt (n + 1) - Real.sqrt n` matches the informal $a_i = \sqrt{i+1} - \sqrt{i}$ exactly (natural number coerced to ℝ inside `Real.sqrt`). The sum `∑ i ∈ range n, g i` is precisely $\sum_{i<n} a_i$ since `range n = {0, 1, ..., n-1}`. The conclusion `Tendsto (fun n => ∑ i ∈ range n, g i) atTop atTop` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "the partial sums tend to ∞ as n → ∞", which is exactly the informal limit claim. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure and ambient types match.
